title: We should fail Podman with ExitCode 125 by default
body:
$ ./bin/podman  --foo
$ echo $?
125
$ ./bin/podman  foo
Command "foo" not found.
See `podman --help`.
$ echo $?
1

After this change

$ ./bin/podman  foo
Command "foo" not found.
See `podman --help`.
$ echo $?
125

Signed-off-by: Daniel J Walsh <dwalsh@redhat.com>

comments.body:
LGTM (verified locally)

Thanks, @rhatdan, for documenting the behavior!
The logs_test periodically inserts a blank line into the log output.
I don't really see any need to make this change, but I'm not opposed either

@mheon The current documentation on podman run is broken, since it says the exit code will be 125 not 1.  We should document the same behavior for podman exec and podman start, since they can get errors back from container commands.

If we exit with 1, a script would not be able to tell the diffence between a command exiting with a 1 exit code inside of the container versus podman failing to execute because the CLI was screwed up.
Maybe we can get https://github.com/containers/libpod/pull/1403 in before to keep the log tests enabled.

Well a miracle happened and we passed test. so we don't need to wait.

@rh-atomic-bot r=vrothberg
